permissions.__comment__: Two callers: (1) backup rotation/listing readDirs $APPCONFIG/backups (explicit entries below; fs:default's recursive app-dir read covers it too); (2) the statements archive (src/lib/statements-archive.ts) readDirs settings.statementsFolderPath — a user-chosen folder PERSISTED across sessions, so the dialog's session-only auto-grant is not enough and the $HOME scope must stay. Narrowing $HOME away silently breaks PDF archiving (it degrades to a warning string, never throws).

Backup rotation (src/lib/backup-restore.ts rotateBackups) deletes the oldest cairn-*.db files beyond MAX_BACKUPS — the ONLY remove caller in the app. Narrowed from $HOME/**/* (Wave 5, 2026-07): the webview can now only delete inside the app's own backups dir.

Static $HOME-scoped write access. Why this exists alongside dialog: tauri-plugin-dialog 2.7.1 auto-grants fs scope after dialog.open() (its commands.rs calls scope.allow_file/allow_directory unconditionally), but that grant is session-only and disappears on app restart. This static scope persists across restarts so files chosen in a prior session remain writable. Caveat: paths outside $HOME (external drives at /Volumes/*, network mounts, etc.) are NOT covered — those still rely on the dialog's session-only grant. Pattern note: '$HOME/**/*' requires at least one path segment under $HOME and does not match $HOME itself, unlike Tauri's canonical '$HOME/**'. Left as-is because we only ever touch files inside subdirectories of $HOME.

runBackup ensures $APPCONFIG/backups exists (recursive mkdir) — the ONLY mkdir caller. Narrowed from $HOME/**/* (Wave 5, 2026-07). $APPCONFIG itself is listed because a recursive mkdir on first run may create the parent; fs:default's create-app-specific-dirs also covers that.
